label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is whether following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. I need to figure out the total displacement after all the steps. First, I always face forward. That means when I take steps backward, I'm moving away from the starting point, and when I take steps forward, I'm moving towards the starting point or past it, depending on the direction. The instructions are: 1. Take 2 steps backward. 2. Take 3 steps backward. 3. Take 3 steps backward. 4. Take 8 steps forward. Let me think of this as movement on a number line where the starting point is 0. Facing forward means that forward is in the positive direction. So, backward steps would be in the negative direction. First step: 2 steps backward = -2 Second step: 3 steps backward = -3, so total displacement is -2 -3 = -5 Third step: another 3 steps backward = -3, so total displacement is -5 -3 = -8 Fourth
